text: Migrate every route and file from the Pages Router to the Next.js App Router. When finished, remove the pages dir entirely. Ensure the proper App Router APIs are used. If a Pages Router API was used that no longer exists in App Router, replace it with the newer version or the new pattern. Make sure to add types.
I’m checking the current Next.js structure first so I can map every `pages` route/file to its App Router equivalent and catch any Pages Router-only APIs that need replacing.

text: The repo is small and currently only has a `pages` tree, so I’m reading each route and shared file now to translate them cleanly into `app` conventions, including API handlers and document/app-level behavior.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "cat package.json && printf '\\n---NEXT---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' next.config.ts && printf '\\n---TS---\\n' && cat tsconfig.json"

text: I’ve identified all Pages Router-only pieces: `_app`, `_document`, `_error`, `404`, route data functions, `next/head`, `next/router`, and API handlers. I’m replacing them with `app` layout/page/error/not-found/route patterns and converting the shared code to typed TypeScript at the same time.
I’m at the edit stage now. The migration will centralize data fetching and types so the new `app` pages and route handlers stay consistent instead of duplicating loose JS shapes across files.

text: The new `app` tree is in place. I’m doing a second pass on types and runtime details now, then I’ll remove `pages` and validate the project with a build so any App Router API mismatches are caught.

I found a couple of App Router-specific cleanup items before verification: some metadata needs to stay on pages/layouts only, and I want the root layout typed without relying on the global `React` namespace. I’m tightening those now and then deleting the legacy `pages` tree.
The legacy router files are removed. I’m running `next build` now to force the full App Router compile path and surface any remaining typing or API issues.

text:
Migrated the project fully to the App Router and removed `pages` entirely. The app now uses `app/layout.tsx`, typed server pages, `app/error.tsx`, `app/not-found.tsx`, and App Router API handlers under `app/api/.../route.ts`.

Key Pages Router replacements are in place: `getServerSideProps` became server component fetching with `headers()`, `getStaticProps`/`getStaticPaths` became `revalidate` plus `generateStaticParams`, `next/head` became `metadata`/`generateMetadata`, `next/router` became `next/navigation` patterns, and API routes now use `NextResponse`. I also converted shared code to TypeScript, added explicit data types in [lib/blog.ts](/home/user/workspace/lib/blog.ts:1), and moved the provider to [components/AppProvider.tsx](/home/user/workspace/components/AppProvider.tsx:1).

Verification: `npm run build` passed. Next updated [tsconfig.json](/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json:1) to include `.next/dev/types/**/*.ts`, which I kept.
